text.title: Agentic Market Sizing Pipeline
text.summary: 7-step agent pipeline, ICP lock rule, prompt patterns, triangulation requirement, and agent gotchas.

rule.statement: Lock the ICP definition in one sentence and wait for explicit confirmation before fetching any number. "All SMBs" is not an ICP.
rule.rationale: Agents computing TAM/SAM/SOM without a locked ICP will scope to different customer segments across the three methods, making triangulation impossible and producing numbers that cannot be compared.

list.item: Step 1 — ICP lock: restate ICP in one sentence, await confirmation
Step 2 — Top-down fetch: WebSearch for "{industry} market size {year}", capture top 3 sources, prefer government/Statista/Gartner; archive each via Wayback
Step 3 — Bottom-up build: count target companies via Census/Eurostat/Apollo, multiply by realistic ARPU
Step 4 — Competitor sweep: pull Crunchbase + SimilarWeb for top 5 competitors, sum + extrapolate long tail (1.5x named players)
Step 5 — Triangulate: run tam-triangulate.sh; write median to report only when spread is 2x or less
Step 6 — Confidence tagging: tag every cell H/M/L based on source primacy and recency (12 months or less = H)
Step 7 — Validation pass: secondary agent re-reads report and returns a delta list

example.code:
Sizing prompt: You are a market sizer. Compute TAM, SAM, SOM for: <niche>. Constraints: <geo, segment, ICP>. Horizon: 3 years. Run THREE methods (top-down, bottom-up, competitor) in parallel. For every number cite the source URL + retrieval date. If methods diverge >2x, do NOT average — return both and flag. Output: markdown matching the Market Sizing Report template.
Validation prompt: Validate this sizing: <numbers + assumptions>. Check: (1) does TAM cite a primary source? (2) is SAM constraint stack independent? (3) is SOM share within Year-N guidelines? Return a delta list, not a rewrite.
example.note: Sizing and validation are separate agent calls with separate prompts. The validator reads the sizing output; it does not regenerate.

antipattern.description: LLMs hallucinate market-size numbers fluently; they require a URL for every figure.
antipattern.reason: Reject any line without a source URL. LLMs generate plausible-sounding numbers (e.g. "$4.2B by 2027") that have no factual basis. The URL is the only enforcement mechanism.

antipattern.description: Confirmation bias: if the user hints at a target market size, the agent will reverse-engineer to that number.
antipattern.reason: Pre-commit the methodology and share the ICP definition before revealing the hypothesis. The agent must compute the number before seeing the founder's expectation.

antipattern.description: Currency drift: agents mix USD, EUR, GBP within one report.
antipattern.reason: Normalize to USD with a dated FX rate in the report preamble. Require the agent to convert all figures before synthesizing.

antipattern.description: Confusing GMV with revenue: marketplaces report GMV; SaaS reports ARR. Adding them is invalid.
antipattern.reason: The agent must identify the revenue metric type for each competitor before including it in the competitor-based sizing. Mixed metrics produce incorrect TAM estimates.

antipattern.description: Stale training data: the model "knows" 2023 market sizes and uses them unless forced to fetch.
antipattern.reason: Force a fresh web fetch for any post-2024 market number. Add an explicit instruction: "Do not use training data for market size figures; always fetch from a cited source."
